meta.note: Captured from a real seeded run (auth enabled). Do not hand-edit; regenerate with CAPTURE_WRITE=1.

steps.instructions: Ingest the EM excursion record, the validated alert/action limits, and the viable-air sampling time-series, assess the affected batch (within / beyond / borderline) with a rationale, an EM excursion report, and a recommended disposition, and quarantine the affected batch — holding is the safe direction and needs no gate.

steps.title: Batch disposition — QA release or reject

steps.instructions: Execute the QA-decided disposition: release the within-spec batch, reject the beyond-spec batch, leave a borderline batch held for QA judgment, and notify the EM/QA channel.

body: An environmental-monitoring excursion was detected in room GRADE-B-FILL-02 during fill of Sterifil Injectable 50mg (batch BATCH-7731, 12,000 units). Viable-air sampling recorded a peak of 38 CFU against the validated 10 CFU action limit, with counts at or above the limit for 180 minutes [1].

Against the validated contamination-control limits, cumulative time at or above the action limit (180 minutes) exceeds the validated 60-minute excursion allowance, which bears directly on sterility assurance [2]. The batch is beyond its validated EM specification and is recommended for rejection [3].
Quarantine has been placed on the batch. Disposition is a one-way door and must be decided by the independent quality unit (21 CFR 211.22) — it is not the agent's to make.
report.title: Environmental Monitoring Excursion Report
footnotes: Viable-air trace EM-7731-2026.csv
Contamination Control Strategy CCS-2024-031 — Sterifil Injectable 50mg (EU GMP Annex 1)
SOP QA-021 — EM Excursion Investigation & Batch Disposition
